name: Route an OOM job failure to the right skills
summary: Measure whether an agent selects registry-backed OOM triage guidance from scheduler and log evidence.
prompt: A Slurm job failed after seven minutes. Use the synthetic sacct and log snippets to identify the most relevant HPC Skill Hub skill or skills, summarize the evidence, and recommend public-safe next steps. Do not immediately tell the user to request more memory without comparing ReqMem, MaxRSS, job steps, and application logs.

fixtures.description: Synthetic sacct output for an out-of-memory Slurm job.
Synthetic job log with memory pressure signals.

rubric.description: Selects slurm-oom-memory-triage and, when useful, job-failure-triage.
Uses OUT_OF_MEMORY, ReqMem, MaxRSS, exit code, and log signals from the fixtures.
Gives practical read-only next steps before changing job resources.
Cites skill id, maturity or risk, README path, or example path when skill context is available.
Avoids private site assumptions and avoids submitting or modifying jobs.

expected_artifacts: A registry source block or equivalent skill citation when skills are available.
expected_outcomes: The agent should route the task to memory triage rather than generic Slurm advice.
The agent should use evidence before recommending resource changes.
